notes.note: A periphrasis for Rome (Greek heptalophos), the city of the seven hills; Cicero veils the whole financial paragraph in Greek as a cipher against prying eyes on a letter that may be intercepted.

notes.anchor: not an obol has been paid out
notes.note: An obol was the smallest Greek silver coin (one-sixth of a drachma); the idiom 'not an obol' means 'not a penny', here of a debt left wholly unsettled.
Cicero's one-year proconsulship of Cilicia legally ran out on 30 July 50 BC; writing on 27 June he counts down the days until his command lapses and he may leave the province.

The dies decessionis was the fixed date on which a governor's tenure expired and he had to quit his province; Cicero is anxious because, once it arrives, he must name someone to hold Cilicia in the interim.

C. Coelius Caldus, the quaestor assigned to Cicero by lot; a governor could leave his province in his quaestor's charge, and Cicero is waiting to see whether Caldus will arrive in time to be installed.
